_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MD equivalent (in General Medicine, Pediatric Medicine)
DNB equivalent (in General Medicine, Pediatric Medicine)
clinical_indications: Diagnosis made is backed by clinical manifestation as per Table 2.8. Signs and symptoms helpful in diagnosis of poisoning, including:

CNS signs (Delirium/hallucinations, Depression/coma, Convulsions, Weakness or paralysis, Fasciculations, Dilated pupil, Small pupil) with associated poisons (e.g., Antihistamines, Barbiturates, Organophosphates, Atropine group, Opium group).
Respiratory signs (Respiratory difficulty, Cyanosis without respiratory distress) with associated poisons (e.g., Organophosphate-insecticides, Methaemoglobinaemia).
Temperature abnormality (High fever, Hypothermia) with associated poisons (e.g., Salicylates, Opiates).
CVS signs (Hypotension, Hypertension, Bradycardia) with associated poisons (e.g., Beta-blockers, Amphetamine, Digitalis).
Odours (e.g., Kerosene, bitter almond-cyanides, garlic-parathion, phosphorus, alcohol, phenols and cresols, sulfides).
Poisoning types include: Aluminum phosphide, Phenol, Hydrocarbons, Carbone monoxide, Ethylene glycol and Methanol, Dhatura poisoning, Opioid intoxication etc.

Copy of Medico legal certificate / FIR

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers with treatment given details

ppd_preauth.q: Was the clinical notes, signs, and symptoms, supporting with laboratory investigations are indicative of poisoning?
Were the Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment submitted?
Was the Red cell cholinesterase level, ECG, CBL, LFT, KFT (as applicable) submitted?
Was the Copy of Medico legal certificate / FIR submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Was the Detailed Indoor case papers with the Treatment details submitted?
Was the Detailed discharge summary submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes, evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, or planned line of treatment not submitted or incomplete.
Red cell cholinesterase level, ECG, CBL, LFT, KFT reports (as applicable) not submitted or incomplete or not justified if applicable.
Copy of Medico legal certificate / FIR not submitted.
Clinical notes, signs, symptoms, or supporting laboratory investigations not indicative of poisoning.
Clinical manifestations for the specific type of poisoning not documented or inconsistent with diagnosis.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed with Poisoning procedure only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical manifestation:
Stepwise care approach to a patient of poisoning is helpful in successful management.
